task_completion.description: Open the portrait image in GIMP
task_completion.visual_evidence: The GIMP application window is open and displays a portrait photograph of a person.

task_completion.description: Navigate to and open the Softglow filter dialog
task_completion.visual_evidence: The 'Softglow' dialog box is visible on screen, typically accessed via Filters -> Light and Shadow -> Softglow.
Inside the Softglow dialog box, the 'Glow radius' setting is adjusted, ideally to a value between 10 and 15 pixels.

task_completion.description: Apply the filter to create a dreamy, luminous effect
task_completion.visual_evidence: The portrait image in the main GIMP canvas shows a visible change: highlights are brighter and glowing, edges are softer, and the overall image has a dreamy, luminous appearance.
An 'Export Image' dialog is visible with the filename set to 'soft_glow_result.jpg', or there is terminal/file manager evidence that this specific file was saved.

integrity.description: The agent used the GIMP graphical user interface to apply the filter
integrity.visual_evidence: Screenshots show interaction with GIMP menus, dialogs, and canvas rather than using command-line image processing tools (like ImageMagick).

integrity.description: The agent actually applied the filter rather than downloading a pre-edited image
integrity.visual_evidence: There is a clear progression from the original sharp portrait to the soft-glow portrait within the GIMP workspace.

integrity.description: The agent did not use a Python script to bypass GUI interaction
integrity.visual_evidence: The terminal does not show the execution of Python scripts (e.g., using PIL or OpenCV) to apply a blur/glow effect and save the image.

privileged_info_for_vlm: The task requires using GIMP. The correct filter path is Filters -> Light and Shadow -> Softglow. The recommended glow radius for the effect is 10-15 pixels. The final image must be exported as 'soft_glow_result.jpg'. A successful application will visually show a 5-20% brightness increase in highlight regions, softer edges (15-40% reduction in edge strength), and visible glow halos around bright areas.
